howItWorks.intro: This mining electricity cost calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Daily Cost = kWh x Electricity Rate
Profit = (Daily BTC x BTC Price) - Daily Power Cost
howItWorks.outro: Cryptocurrency markets are highly volatile. Past performance does not guarantee future results.

faq.question: How much electricity does a Bitcoin miner use?
faq.answer: Modern ASIC miners like the Antminer S21 draw 3,000-3,500 watts and produce 100-200 TH/s. Running 24/7, that is 72-84 kWh per day or about 2,200-2,500 kWh per month. At $0.10/kWh, monthly electricity runs $220-250 per unit.

faq.question: What electricity rate makes mining profitable?
faq.answer: Profitability depends heavily on BTC price and network difficulty. Generally, rates below $0.07/kWh are competitive for Bitcoin mining. Industrial miners often locate near cheap hydroelectric or stranded natural gas at $0.03-0.05/kWh.

faq.question: Is the Mining Electricity Cost Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard cryptocurrency and blockchain formulas trusted by crypto investors and traders. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What hash rate should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate hash rate value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Mining Electricity Cost Calculator?
sections.body: The Mining Electricity Cost Calculator is a free online tool designed for crypto investors and traders who need quick, accurate calculations in the cryptocurrency and blockchain space. By entering your hash rate, power consumption, electricity rate, you get instant results including daily power cost, monthly power cost, daily revenue. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting daily power cost right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In cryptocurrency and blockchain, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by crypto investors and traders worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to evaluate your crypto portfolio with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your hash rate and need to find the right daily power cost. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Hash Rate (TH/s)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your hash rate — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: power consumption, electricity rate, btc price, est. daily btc earned. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your daily power cost, monthly power cost, daily revenue. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your hash rate before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both daily power cost and monthly power cost — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to evaluate your crypto portfolio.
If you're unsure about your est. daily btc earned, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
